content: A plane electromagnetic wave with frequency 40 MHz travels in free space. At a particular point in space and time, the magnetic field is $2 \times 10^{-8} T$. What will be the electric field at this point?
explanation:
The electric and magnetic fields in a free-space plane wave are related by

$$E = c\,B$$

where $c = 3\times10^8\rm\ m/s$. Plugging in $B = 2\times10^{-8}\rm\ T$,

$$E = (3\times10^8)(2\times10^{-8}) = 6\ \rm V/m\,. $$

So the correct choice is Option B: 6 V/m.

content: The electric and magnetic fields associated with an electromagnetic wave propagating along +z axis, can be represented by
explanation:
For a wave travelling along +z, the fields must satisfy

$E\perp B$,

$E\times B$ points in the +z‐direction (that’s the Poynting vector).

Checking each option:

Option A: $E=E_0\,\hat i,\;B=B_0\,\hat j$ gives

$ E\times B = E_0B_0\,(\hat i\times\hat j)=E_0B_0\,\hat k $

which is +z.

All other choices give the wrong propagation direction.

Answer: Option A.

content:
Column - I lists the waves of the electromagnetic spectrum. Column - II gives approximate frequency range of these waves. Match Column - I and Column - II and choose the correct match from the given choices.

Column I Column II
(A) Radiowaves (P) $$
10^{18} \text { to } 10^{20} \mathrm{~Hz}
$$
(B) Microwaves (P) $$
10^{11} \text { to } 5 \times 10^{14} \mathrm{~Hz}
$$
(C) Infrared (R) $$
10^4 \text { to } 10^8 \mathrm{~Hz}
$$
(D) X-rays (S) $$
10^9 \text { to } 10^{12} \mathrm{~Hz}
$$
An electromagnetic wave of frequency $$3 \mathrm{~MHz}$$ passes from vacuum into a dielectric medium $$(\mu_r=1)$$ of relative permittivity 4.0. Then,

options.content: Wavelength is halved and frequency is doubled
Wavelength doubled and frequency remains unchanged
Wavelength is halved and frequency remains unchanged

explanation:
When an electromagnetic wave passes from one medium to another, the frequency remains unchanged. Here's why:

In vacuum, the speed of light is $$c$$ and the wavelength is given by the formula:

$$\lambda_0 = \frac{c}{f}$$

When the wave enters the dielectric medium, its speed becomes:

$$v = \frac{c}{\sqrt{\epsilon_r \mu_r}}$$

Given that $$\mu_r = 1$$ and $$\epsilon_r = 4.0$$, we have:

$$v = \frac{c}{\sqrt{4.0}} = \frac{c}{2}$$

The frequency $$f$$ remains the same as in vacuum, so the new wavelength $$\lambda'$$ in the medium is:

$$\lambda' = \frac{v}{f} = \frac{c/2}{f} = \frac{1}{2}\left(\frac{c}{f}\right) = \frac{\lambda_0}{2}$$

Thus, the wavelength is halved, and the frequency remains unchanged.

The correct option is:

Option D

Wavelength is halved and frequency remains unchanged.

content: The ratio of amplitude of magnetic field to the amplitude of electric field of an electromagnetic wave propagating in vacuum is

options.content: reciprocal of speed of light in vacuum
the speed of light in vacuum
proportional to frequency of the electromagnetic wave
inversely proportional to the frequency of the electromagnetic wave

explanation:
For an electromagnetic wave, Speed of electromagnetic wave, $$c=\frac{E_0}{B_0}$$

$$\therefore \frac{B_0}{E_0}=\frac{1}{c}=$$ reciprocal of speed

content: A plane electromagnetic wave of frequency $$20 \mathrm{~MHz}$$ travels through a space along $$x$$-direction. If the electric field vector at a certain point in space is $$6 \mathrm{~Vm}^{-1}$$, then what is the magnetic field vector at that point?
What feature of the infrared waves make it useful for the haze photography?

options.content: Since it has large wave length
Since it is absorbed by the medium

explanation:
The feature of infrared waves that makes them useful for haze photography is Option B: Since it has large wavelength.

Infrared radiation has a longer wavelength than visible light. This characteristic allows it to penetrate atmospheric haze more effectively than the shorter wavelengths of visible light. Haze and fog are composed of small particles like water droplets which scatter visible light much more than infrared radiation. Since the longer wavelengths of infrared light are not scattered as much, they can pass through the haze and reach the camera. This ability to cut through atmospheric haze and fog enables photographers to capture clearer images with greater contrast and detail over long distances, which would not be possible with regular visible light photography.

The options A, C, and D do not accurately describe the feature that makes infrared wavelengths specifically useful for haze photography:

  • Option A: Invisibility is not the key factor; the ability to penetrate atmospheric particulates like haze plays the critical role.
  • Option C: The absorption by medium (like atmospheric gases) affects all electromagnetic waves to some extent, but it's not the reason why infrared is chosen for cutting through haze.
  • Option D: Infrared waves actually have a lower frequency compared to visible light, not higher. Frequency inversely relates to the wavelength; hence, higher wavelength equates to a lower frequency in the electromagnetic spectrum.

content: Speed of electromagnetic wave in a medium having relative permittivity $$\varepsilon_r$$ and relative permeability $$\mu_r$$ is (speed of light in air, $$c=3\times10^8$$ m/s)
explanation:
Speed of EM wave in medium is given as

$$v = {c \over {\sqrt {{\mu _r}{\varepsilon _r}} }}$$

content:
A 30 mW laser beam has a cross-sectional area of 15 mm2. The magnitude of the maximum electric field in this electromagnetic wave is given by

[Permuittivity of space, $$\varepsilon_0=9\times10^{-12}$$

Speed of light, $$c=3\times10^8$$ m/s]
explanation:
Given, power of laser beam,

$$P=30~mW=30\times10^{-3}~W$$

Area of cross section, A = 15 mm$$^2$$ = 15 $$\times$$ 10$$^{-6}$$ m$$^2$$

Permittivity of free space, $$\varepsilon_0=9\times10^{-12}$$ SI unit

Speed of light, $$c=3\times10^8$$ m/s

Intensity of electromagnetic wave is given by

$$I=\frac{1}{2}nc\varepsilon_0E^2$$

where, $$n$$ is refractive index for air, $$n=1$$

$$I=\frac{1}{2}c~.~\varepsilon_0E^2$$ ..... (i)

$$I=\frac{P}{A}$$ ....... (ii)

From Eqs. (i) and (ii), we get

$${1 \over 2}c{\varepsilon _0}{E^2} = {P \over A}$$

$${E^2} = {{2P} \over {Ac{\varepsilon _0}}}$$

$$E = \sqrt {{{2 \times 30 \times {{10}^{ - 3}}} \over {15 \times {{10}^{ - 6}} \times 3 \times {{10}^8} \times 9 \times {{10}^{ - 12}}}}} $$

$$ = 1.22 \times {10^3}$$ V/m = 1.22 kV/m

content: The correct arrangement in increasing order of wavelength of X-rays, UV rays, microwave is
explanation:
The increasing order of frequency is Microwave < UV rays < X-rays

The frequency is the inverse of wavelength. So, the order of increasing wavelength is

X-rays < UV rays < Microwave

content: Which of the following waves are used to treatment of muscles ache?
explanation: Infrared rays are used for the treatment of muscles ache. Due to their heating properties they are used in infrared therapy.

content: Which of the following has/have zero average value in a plane electromagnetic wave?
explanation: Both magnet and electric fields have zero average value in a plane electromagnetic wave.
